summary: Split Yoshi special-move code that had been bundled under `ftYs_SpecialHi.c`, added and linked a matching `ftYs_SpecialLw.c`, introduced a separate nonmatching `ftYs_SpecialS.c`, and cleaned related Yoshi headers/types/inlines. The decomp.dev report showed linked code +1284 bytes and 27 new matches, mostly functions moved into the new `ftYs_SpecialLw` and `ftYs_SpecialS` units; the reported broken matches were the same functions leaving the old `ftYs_SpecialHi` unit, so they appear to be split relocation effects rather than regressions.
what_changed: Updated GALE01 splits so `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` ends at `0x8012E644`, new `ftYs_SpecialLw.c` covers `0x8012E644..0x8012EB48`, and new `ftYs_SpecialS.c` covers `0x8012EB48..0x80132A0C`.
Added `ftYs_SpecialLw.c` as a matching object in `configure.py`; added `ftYs_SpecialS.c` as nonmatching so the remaining Yoshi side-special range is isolated but not claimed matching.
Moved Yoshi down-special implementation out of `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` into new `ftYs_SpecialLw.c`, including enter/anim/phys/coll/landing helpers and the star-spawn accessory callback `fn_8012E644`.

Created `ftYs_SpecialS.c` and `ftYs_SpecialS.h` for Yoshi side-special functions that had previously lived in the `ftYs_SpecialHi` source range, preserving many functions as documented nonmatching stubs while matching several IASA/phys/coll leaf functions.

Cleaned `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` includes and helper naming, gave `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C` a concrete signature `void (Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*)`, and replaced raw motion-state constants in some paths with named `ftYs_MS_*` and `motion_flags`.
Removed unrelated SpecialHi/SpecialLw/SpecialS prototypes from `ftYs_SpecialN.h` and deleted `ftYs_SpecialN.static.h`, moving its static prototypes into `ftYs_SpecialN.c`.

Extended `ftYs_DatAttrs` with offsets used by SpecialHi and SpecialLw: `xEC`, `xF0`, `xF4`, `specialhi_base_angle`, `xFC`, `x100`, and `speciallw_star_offset`, with a static size assertion of `0x120`.
Added small shared inline helpers in `ft/inlines.h`: `getStickDirX(Fighter*)` and `ftGetFacingDirInt(Fighter*)`.
Changed four Yoshi `.sdata2` symbols at `0x804D9A58..0x804D9A64` from global `ftYs_Init_*` names to local `@183`, `@184`, `@194`, and `@246`, matching their local constant usage in the split unit.
smart_moves: Treated the PR as a linker/split cleanup: first narrowed the SpecialHi range, then introduced separate SpecialLw and SpecialS compilation units so future matching work can target smaller, semantically coherent files.
Kept `ftYs_SpecialLw.c` fully matching while leaving `ftYs_SpecialS.c` nonmatching, avoiding overclaiming progress for the larger side-special body.

Converted repeated facing-direction branch logic into `ftGetFacingDirInt`, which let `ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Coll` express the original check succinctly while preserving the integer `-1/+1` behavior needed by ledge/ground helpers.

Added `ftYs_DatAttrs` fields at exact offsets instead of continuing to rely only on anonymous padding, allowing SpecialHi angle math and SpecialLw star offsets to be written against typed data attributes.
Used named motion-state IDs and named bitwise motion flags where possible, improving readability without changing the matching-sensitive flag value.
Cleaned header ownership by moving SpecialHi/Lw/S declarations out of `ftYs_SpecialN.h`, reducing accidental cross-file declarations after the split.
decomp_lessons: When a source file is carrying several adjacent assembly ranges, split `splits.txt` and `configure.py` together; otherwise decomp.dev may report apparent broken matches in the old unit even though the code was successfully relocated.
A bot report with many 'broken matches' in the old object and corresponding 'new matches' in new objects can indicate a successful file split, not a functional regression.
For fighter special-move code, keep per-move headers focused: `SpecialN.h` should not declare unrelated `SpecialHi`, `SpecialLw`, or `SpecialS` functions once those units exist.
Adding typed dat-attribute fields at known offsets can unlock cleaner matching code for math-heavy routines such as angle/vector calculation and item spawn offsets.
Small inlines for common sign/facing decisions are reusable matching aids, especially when the original code wants integer `-1/+1` rather than the fighter's float `facing_dir`.
Local `.sdata2` constants may need to be renamed from placeholder globals to local `@NNN` symbols when code is split into the unit that actually owns them.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter files use the `ftYs_` prefix and move-specific filenames such as `ftYs_SpecialN.c`, `ftYs_SpecialHi.c`, `ftYs_SpecialLw.c`, and `ftYs_SpecialS.c`.
Motion-state names are preferred over raw IDs where known, e.g. `ftYs_MS_SpecialLw`, `ftYs_MS_SpecialAirLw`, `ftYs_MS_SpecialLwLanding`, and `ftYs_MS_SpecialAirHi`.

Helper names introduced in this PR describe intent rather than placeholder names: `setAccessory4Callback`, `setDamageCallbacks`, `setupCallbacks`, `checkAnimEnd`, `changeMotionState`, `getStickDirX`, and `ftGetFacingDirInt`.
Unknown Yoshi attribute fields remain offset-named (`xEC`, `xF0`, `xF4`, `xFC`, `x100`) while understood fields get semantic names (`specialhi_base_angle`, `speciallw_star_offset`).
Private helpers in new units are declared `static` or `static inline` when they are local to that compilation unit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8` in small functions where stack shape was matching-sensitive, including SpecialLw enter/anim/phys routines and SpecialN helper cleanup.

Preserved exact motion flags through bitwise named constants such as `Ft_MF_SkipHit | Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim | Ft_MF_SkipColAnim | Ft_MF_UpdateCmd | Ft_MF_SkipItemVis | Ft_MF_Unk19 | Ft_MF_SkipModelPartVis | Ft_MF_SkipModelFlags | Ft_MF_Unk27` for a SpecialLw motion transition.

For `fn_8012E644`, spawned two Yoshi stars by reading `FtPart_TransN` world position with `lb_8000B1CC`, applying mirrored `speciallw_star_offset.x`, calling `it_802B2FC8` with `-1.0f` and `+1.0f`, and clearing `accessory4_cb`.
For SpecialHi vector math, typed `ftYs_DatAttrs` enabled code using stick magnitude, `specialhi_base_angle`, `cosf`, and `sinf` to produce the velocity vector for `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C`.

Changed static inline functions in SpecialN cleanup to `static` functions where appropriate, likely to control inlining/codegen during the cleanup; evidence is diff-only, no reviewer comment explained the intent.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in this PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improved to 38.91% (+0.01%, +316 bytes) and linked code improved to 25.71% (+0.03%, +1284 bytes).

decomp.dev bot listed 27 new matches, including 16 functions in `ftYs_SpecialLw` and multiple leaf functions in `ftYs_SpecialS`; it also listed 25 broken matches in `ftYs_SpecialHi`, corresponding to functions moved out of that unit.
decomp.dev bot reported one unmatched-function improvement: `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C` in `ftYs_SpecialHi` improved to 96.03%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that make `ftYs_SpecialS.c` matching after this split.
Search for later renames of `ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04`, `ftYs_SpecialS_8012EAD8`, and `fn_8012E644` to semantic Yoshi down-special names.
Check whether `ftYs_DatAttrs` fields `xEC`, `xF0`, `xF4`, `xFC`, and `x100` receive semantic names in later Yoshi work.
Look for reuse or modification of `ftGetFacingDirInt` and `getStickDirX` in other fighter decomp PRs.
Verify whether the local `.sdata2` constants `@183`, `@184`, `@194`, and `@246` are later named or moved.
